structural_claim: Continuous systems spontaneously organize into discrete, periodic structures through symmetry-breaking instabilities when competing forces reach critical thresholds

supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Rotating flows spontaneously break symmetry to form discrete vortex structures when centrifugal forces overcome viscous damping
Thermal convection self-organizes into discrete cellular patterns when buoyancy forces overcome viscous damping
Wake flows spontaneously organize into periodic vortex patterns with specific spacing ratios
Wave propagation in periodic structures naturally organizes into discrete bands and gaps
Standing waves create discrete potential wells through interference patterns
